title: dalbergia monetaria var. hygrophila
intro: Dalbergia monetaria var. hygrophila, also known as Indian rosewood, is a tropical tree species native to the Western Ghats of India. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of Dalbergia monetaria var. hygrophila typically involve making teas or infusions from its leaves or bark, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
